Version.Name: Carcinogenicity model (CAESAR)
Version.Summary: QSAR classification model for Carcinogenicity (from CAESAR project)

Guide.Description: The model provides a qualitative prediction of carcinogenic potency according to specific requirements of Chemical regulation. It is implemented inside the VEGA online platform, accessible at:http://www.vega-qsar.eu/. TThe model extends the original CAESAR model, freely available at: http://www.caesar-project.eu/software/
Guide.Model: The model has been built as a Counter Propagation Artificial Neural Network (CP ANN). The neural network output consists of two values labeled as Positive and Non-Positive, both in the range [0,1] and with sum equal to 1; they represent how much the neuron in which the predicted compound falls belongs to the class of carcinogenic or non-carcinogenic compounds. The higher between these two values leads to the prediction. Full reference and details of the used formulas can be found in: Fjodorova N., Vračko M., Novič M., Roncaglioni A. and Benfenati E. New public QSAR model for carcinogenicity. Chemistry Central Journal (2010), 4 (Suppl 1):S3
Guide.Descriptors: The descriptors used are the following: - PW5: Path/walk 5 - Randic shape index - D/Dr6: Distance/detour ring index of order 6 - MATS2: Moran autocorrelation - lag 2/weighted by atomic polarizabilities - EEig10: Eigenvalue 10 from edge adj. matrix weighted by edge degrees - ESpm11: Spectral moment 11 from edge adj. matrix weighted by edge degrees - ESpm9: Spectral moment 09 from edge adj. matrix weighted by dipole moments - GGI2: Topological charge index of order 2 - JGI6: Mean topological charge index of order 6 - nRNNOx: Number of N-nitroso groups (aliphatic) - nPO4: Number of phosphates/thiophosphates - N_067: Number of Al2-NH atom centered fragments - N_078: Number of Ar-N = X/X-N = X atom centered fragments

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: Reliability
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: model class assignment is well defined

AD.Name: NMN_concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted value agrees with experimental values of training set compounds laying in the same neuron
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted value disagrees with experimental values of training set compounds laying in the same neuron
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance falls into a neuron that is populated by no compounds of the training set

AD.Name: Model_Descriptors_Range_Check
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: descriptors for this compound have values inside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: descriptors for this compound have values outside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model
